channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Vornado space heaters recalled for potential shock and fire hazards
item.description: The potential danger stems from power cords that may become partially detached from the heater enclosure.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Steve Bannon's border wall trial delayed until March 4 as new lawyers plot aggressive defense
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon’s trial on charges that he duped donors who gave money to build a wall along the U.S. southern border will start a week later than scheduled, a judge said Wednesday, after the conservative rabble-rouser hired new lawyers to pursue an aggressive defense strategy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, the far-right extremist group leader convicted of seditious conspiracy in the Jan. 6, 2021, attack, visited Capitol Hill on Wednesday after President Donald Trump commuted his 18-year prison sentence.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lynn Ban, star of Netflix reality show, dies at 52 after emergency surgery
item.description: Lynn Ban, who starred in Netflix's reality show "Bling Empire: New York," has died weeks after a skiing accident and emergency brain surgery.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Teen fatally shoots a female student and himself at Antioch High School in Nashville, police say
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A female student was killed and another student was wounded Wednesday in a shooting in a Nashville high school cafeteria, police said.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams: I'm 'at a loss' on how to handle migrants in NYC
item.description: Adams criticized the Biden administration for their border policies in an interview with Tucker Carlson.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Texas man who tried to scam ex-US Rep. George Santos sentenced to 18 months in prison
item.description: A Texas man has been sentenced to a year and a half in prison over his failed effort to dupe disgraced ex-congressman George Santos out of nearly $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump began his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient U.S. government priorities.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Las Vegas man says he ‘accidentally’ shot girlfriend in head before driving with body for almost 2 hours: police
item.description: A man who drove around with his dead girlfriend in his passenger seat for hours asked his friend, who called 911 to report him, to help him get rid of her body in the desert after he had "accidentally" shot her in the back of the head, police documents said.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pardoned violent Jan. 6 rioters. Here are 5 of them
item.description: Hundreds of rioters accused of violently assaulting police at the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, were pardoned Monday by President Trump, despite remarks from his allies in recent weeks condemning those defendants.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas legislation considers AI rules for health insurance claims
item.description: Sen. Charles Schwertner, R-Georgetown, filed SB 815 to stop health insurance companies from using AI to delay, deny or modify claims.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Al-Shaikh, NewsNation

item.title: School districts address possible ICE actions after new immigration crackdown begins
item.description: Denver Public Schools has told its staff, “do not allow entry into the building" if ICE agents show up on campus.
item.dc:creator: Vicente Arenas, NewsNation

item.title: Previously approved refugee flights to US canceled after Trump order
item.description: The U.S. refugee program is considered one of the most rigorous on the globe, providing a pathway to safe haven for those fleeing danger or who has faced persecution due to their race, religion, nationality or political views.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KRON4

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to probe local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration, according to a memo to the workforce obtained by
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Thousands of Vornado space heaters recalled over potential 'shock and fire' hazards
item.description: Thousands of Vornado space heaters sold on Amazon have been recalled amid concerns over possible "electric shock and fire hazards," according to the company.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WGN 9

item.title: An estimated 24.6 million watched the inauguration, the smallest audience since 2013
item.description: The Nielsen Company said viewership was down from Joe Biden's 2021 inauguration, which reached 33.8 million, and Trump's first move into the White House, seen by 30.6 million in 2017.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Historic Deep South snow: The science behind it
item.description: Parts of the Gulf Coast measured a foot of snow on Tuesday. For many cities the totals obliterate long-standing snowfall records.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Berardelli, KRON4

item.title: Cat mistakenly left in plane cargo hold makes 3 trips in 24 hours
item.description: A Maine coon cat named Mittens became an accidental jetsetter this month when her cage was overlooked in a plane cargo hold and she made three trips in 24 hours between New Zealand and Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's immigration crackdown leaves some families weighing risk of sending kids to school
item.description: Fears intensified for some when the Trump administration announced Tuesday it would allow federal immigration agencies to make arrests at schools, churches and hospitals, ending a decades-old policy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday issued an executive order directing the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to "return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring" and eliminate diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alaska leaders cheer Trump oil and gas drilling executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order to boost oil drilling, mining, and logging in Alaska was met with enthusiasm by some state leaders and worry by environmental groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at school. Some families are now weighing school attendance
item.description: As President Donald Trump cracks down on immigrants in the U.S. illegally, some families are wondering if it is safe to send their children to school.
item.dc:creator: Olga R. Rodriguez, Moriah Balingit, Bianca Vázquez Toness And Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Winter storm spreads across the Deep South, creating icy danger and snowy fun
item.description: The winter storm spreading snow, sleet and freezing rain across the southern United States has broken records, treating the region to unaccustomed perils and seasonal joy.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Herbert And David J. Phillip, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bitter cold spreads across much of the US as Texas and the South brace for rare winter storm
item.description: Frigid temperatures engulfed the South on Monday ahead of a winter storm that's expected to spread heavy snow and disruptive ice around a region from Texas to north Florida that rarely sees such weather, sending residents rushing to insulate pipes, check heating systems and stock up on emergency sup
item.dc:creator: SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio State VS. Notre Dame: Facts to know before the CFP Championship kickoff
item.description: The Buckeyes and Fighting Irish meet Monday night in the College Football Playoff national championship game at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate passes Laken Riley Act in first move after Trump inauguration
item.description: The Senate on Monday passed the Laken Riley Act, making the immigration-related bill the first piece of legislation to make it through the upper chamber in the new Congress and putting it a step closer to being signed into law by President Trump. Senators voted 64-35 on the bill.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Planters hiring latest class of 'Nutmobile' drivers
item.description: Mr. Peanut is hiring. Planters is ready to bring in the 2025 class of brand ambassadors and drivers of their 26-foot peanut on wheels, the Nutmobile.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Costco workers authorize nationwide strike
item.description: Thousands of Costco workers nationwide voted Sunday to authorize a strike if a contract is not reached by the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5
